title: penstemon procerus subsp. tolmiei
intro: Penstemon procerus subsp. tolmiei, also known as Tolmie's penstemon, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the figwort family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Tolmie's penstemon can be consumed as a tea, made by infusing the dried leaves and flowers in hot water, or used as a tincture to extract its medicinal properties.
